system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,h)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,R)      Gauss upwind;
    div(R)          Gauss linear;
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p_rgh
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    p_rghFinal
    {
        $p_rgh;
        relTol          0;
    }

    rho
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0;
    }

    rhoFinal
    {
        $rho;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|R)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|R)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    3;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.buoyantFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
keyword p0 is undefined in dictionary "/pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_20260516_194747_run1/runs/BernardCells/3/0/p/boundaryField/topWall"

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_20260516_194747_run1/runs/BernardCells/3/0/p/boundaryField/topWall from line 18 to line 20.

    From function const Foam::entry& Foam::dictionary::lookupEntry(const Foam::word&, bool, bool) const
    in file db/dictionary/dictionary.C at line 831.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<review_analysis># Error Diagnosis and Resolution

## Error Analysis

The error is:

```
keyword p0 is undefined in dictionary ".../0/p/boundaryField/topWall"
```

This means the `buoyantFoam` solver is looking for a boundary condition entry called `p0` (the pressure flux term) in the `topWall` boundary of the `p` field, but it cannot find it.

In OpenFOAM's `buoyantFoam` (OpenFOAM 10), the pressure field `p` is solved and the boundary condition type `totalPressure` requires a `p0` entry (the pressure offset or reference value) in the boundary field specification. The current `0/p` file uses `totalPressure` but does not include the `p0` keyword.

## Proposed Solution

**Modify `0/p`** to include the `p0` keyword in each `totalPressure` boundary condition entry. The `totalPressure` BC type expects `p0` (a scalar value representing the pressure offset) to be specified.

For example, update the `topWall` entry in `0/p` from:

```
topWall
{
    type            totalPressure;
    value           uniform 1e5;
    gradient        uniform 0;
}
```

to:

```
topWall
{
    type            totalPressure;
    p0              uniform 1e5;
    value           uniform 1e5;
    gradient        uniform 0;
}
```

And similarly update **all six patches** (`topWall`, `bottomWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`) in `0/p` to include `p0 uniform 1e5;`. The `p0` entry should specify the same pressure value (`1e5`) used throughout the domain.

This is consistent with how `buoyantFoam` in OpenFOAM 10 handles `totalPressure` boundary conditions — the `p0` keyword is required to anchor the absolute pressure level, since `totalPressure` computes `p + p0` internally and needs to know the reference value. Without `p0`, the solver crashes when it tries to access this undefined keyword.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Perform a 3D Bernard Cell simulation using OpenFOAM buoyantFoam solver. The computational domain spans 9 m x 1 m x 2 m. The simulation begins at t=0 seconds and runs until t = 1000 seconds with a time step of 1 second, and results are written at intervals of every 50 seconds. One wall has a temperature of 310 K, while the other has a temperature of 300 K.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='0/p', changes='Add p0 uniform 1e5; to the topWall totalPressure boundary condition; Add p0 uniform 1e5; to the bottomWall totalPressure boundary condition; Add p0 uniform 1e5; to the leftWall totalPressure boundary condition; Add p0 uniform 1e5; to the rightWall totalPressure boundary condition; Add p0 uniform 1e5; to the frontWall totalPressure boundary condition; Add p0 uniform 1e5; to the backWall totalPressure boundary condition')]
